verdict_reasoning: BAX is a canonical cytosolic pro-apoptotic BCL-2 family member. Its biological activity is exclusively intracellular — MOM permeabilization and oligomerization at the mitochondrial outer membrane during apoptosis. It has no documented TM domain, GPI anchor, stable surface-anchoring partner, secretory pathway routing, or cell-state-induced surface display. Passive exposure during PM rupture in late apoptosis is a terminal cell-death consequence, not evidence of intact-cell surface accessibility. No therapeutic modality targets BAX on intact cell surfaces.
